topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment was thick, suffocating. Liam stared at the crumpled invitation lying on the coffee table, the ink of the planned celebration mocking him with its cheerful promises. His stomach tightened into a hard knot. He re-read the cancellation notice for the fifth time, his jaw clenching so tightly his molars ached. He could almost hear the laughter, the clinking glasses, the sheer joyful chaos that was supposed to erupt in his living room. A slow, hot flush crept up his neck. He picked up his phone, his thumb hovering over a contact, a bitter taste rising in his mouth. He watched a quick, blurry video of Sarah and Mark laughing—a shared moment he wasn't part of—and felt a distinct, sharp ache behind his ribs.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Marcus muttered, the word barely escaping his lips. He paced the length of the narrow hallway, his footsteps echoing hollowly against the polished wood floor. He stopped abruptly by the window, peering out at the street below where other lights twinkled, suggesting a gathering somewhere else. He ran a hand through his hair, feeling the sudden dampness of sweat on his palms. He imagined the scene: the vibrant colors, the carefully chosen music, the focused attention of everyone converging on one person. A strange, cold pressure settled in his chest, a feeling of being left out in the cold. He slumped onto the nearest armchair, staring blankly at the wall, wishing the air itself would shift and somehow include the warmth of that anticipated celebration.

[story 3]

"No way," Chloe whispered, her voice tight with disbelief as she reread the email. Her hands started to tremble slightly, the paper crinkling under her grip. She looked over at the empty space on the dining table where the cake was supposed to sit, and a deep, hollow feeling washed over her. She felt a distinct urge to check her own calendar, to see what she could possibly be doing that was more important than this sudden deflation. A sharp, almost painful yearning surfaced—a desire to be the center of attention, to be the reason for such a grand occasion, and the reality of the situation made the desire feel particularly acute and unwelcome.
